post.title: University of Sydney Scholarship for Asylum Seekers in Australia and International Students from Canada
post.excerpt: Introduction to University of Sydney Scholarship The University of Sydney offers various scholarships to asylum seekers in Australia and international students
post.metaDescription: Introduction to University of Sydney Scholarship The University of Sydney offers various scholarships to asylum seekers in Australia and international stud
post.content:
## Introduction to University of Sydney Scholarship
The University of Sydney offers various scholarships to asylum seekers in Australia and international students from Canada, covering undergraduate and graduate degrees. These scholarships aim to support students who are facing financial difficulties in pursuing their higher education. The University of Sydney Scholarship for asylum seekers and international students provides an opportunity for students to study at one of the world's top universities.

## Who Qualifies
To be eligible for the University of Sydney Scholarship, asylum seekers in Australia and international students from Canada must meet specific visa requirements. The following visa categories are eligible:
* Asylum seeker visa (subclass 866)
* Temporary Protection visa (subclass 785)
* Safe Haven Enterprise visa (subclass 790)
* Permanent Protection visa (subclass 866)
* Student visa (subclass 500) for international students from Canada

## Scholarships Available
The University of Sydney offers several scholarships to support asylum seekers and international students. Here are five available scholarships:
### Sydney Scholars Award
The Sydney Scholars Award provides $6,000 to $10,000 per annum for undergraduate students. To be eligible, students must have achieved an ATAR of 95 or above. The deadline for application is November 2026. For more information, visit https://www.sydney.edu.au/scholarships/sydney-scholars-award.html.
### University of Sydney Access Scheme
The University of Sydney Access Scheme offers $2,000 to $5,000 per annum for undergraduate students. Eligible students must be from a low socio-economic background. The deadline for application is November 2026. For more information, visit https://www.sydney.edu.au/access-scheme.
### Australia Awards Scholarship
The Australia Awards Scholarship provides full tuition fees and living costs for undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, students must be from a developing country, including Canada. The deadline for application is April 2027. For more information, visit https://www.australiaawards.gov.au.
### Canada-Australia Student Mobility Program
The Canada-Australia Student Mobility Program offers $5,000 to $10,000 per annum for undergraduate and graduate students. Eligible students must be from Canada and studying at the University of Sydney. The deadline for application is November 2026. For more information, visit https://www.studyinaustralia.gov.au.
### Humanitarian Scholarship
The Humanitarian Scholarship provides full tuition fees and living costs for undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, students must be asylum seekers or refugees. The deadline for application is November 2026. For more information, visit https://www.sydney.edu.au/humanitarian-scholarship.

## Application Steps
To apply for the University of Sydney Scholarship, follow these steps:
1. Check your eligibility and visa requirements.
2. Choose your desired scholarship and program.
3. Prepare your documents, including academic transcripts and language proficiency test results.
4. Submit your application through the University of Sydney's online portal.
5. Pay the application fee, if required.
6. Wait for the application to be processed and reviewed.
7. Receive notification of the outcome and follow the next steps.
8. Accept the offer and enroll in the program.

## Documents Checklist
To apply for the University of Sydney Scholarship, you will need to provide the following documents:
* Academic transcripts
* Language proficiency test results (e.g. IELTS or TOEFL)
* Passport and visa documents
* Proof of financial support
* Letters of recommendation
* Personal statement and research proposal (for graduate students)

## Frequently Asked Questions
1. What is the eligibility criteria for the University of Sydney Scholarship?
The eligibility criteria vary depending on the scholarship, but generally, students must meet specific visa requirements, academic achievements, and language proficiency.
2. How do I apply for the University of Sydney Scholarship?
To apply, submit your application through the University of Sydney's online portal and provide the required documents.
3. What is the deadline for the University of Sydney Scholarship application?
The deadline varies depending on the scholarship, but generally, it is November 2026 or April 2027.
4. Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the University of Sydney?
Yes, you can apply for multiple scholarships, but you can only receive one award.
5. How long does it take to process the University of Sydney Scholarship application?
The processing time varies, but generally, it takes several weeks to months.
6. Can I defer my University of Sydney Scholarship?
Yes, you can defer your scholarship, but you must submit a request and provide supporting documents.
7. How do I accept my University of Sydney Scholarship offer?
To accept your offer, follow the instructions provided in your notification letter and enroll in the program.
8. What is the contact information for the University of Sydney Scholarship team?
For more information and queries, visit the University of Sydney's official website or contact the scholarship team through their online portal.

For more information on scholarships and visa requirements, visit the [Australian Government Department of Home Affairs](https://www.homeaffairs.gov.au) and the [University of Sydney](https://www.sydney.edu.au) official websites.
To learn more about scholarship opportunities and application processes, visit our blog at https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.

related.title: Australia and Canada's Joint Scholarship Initiative for Refugee Students Pursuing STEM Fields
related.excerpt: Introduction to the Joint Scholarship Initiative The Australian and Canadian governments have launched a joint scholarship initiative for refugee students pursu
related.metaDescription: Introduction to the Joint Scholarship Initiative The Australian and Canadian governments have launched a joint scholarship initiative for refugee students
related.content:
## Introduction to the Joint Scholarship Initiative
The Australian and Canadian governments have launched a joint scholarship initiative for refugee students pursuing STEM fields, providing an opportunity for them to further their education and build a better future. This initiative is specifically designed for refugee students who are looking to study Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics in Australia or Canada. The initiative aims to support the education and career development of refugee students in these fields, which are in high demand globally.

## Who Qualifies
To be eligible for the joint scholarship initiative, refugee students must hold a valid visa in one of the following categories:
* Permanent Resident Visa (subclass 201)
* Refugee and Humanitarian Visa (subclass 200)
* Temporary Protection Visa (subclass 785)
* Safe Haven Enterprise Visa (subclass 790)
* Protection Visa (subclass 866)
* Permanent Resident Visa (Canada)
* Protected Person Visa (Canada)
* Convention Refugee Visa (Canada)

## Available Scholarships
The joint scholarship initiative offers a range of scholarships for refugee students pursuing STEM fields. Some of the available scholarships include:
### Australian Awards Scholarship
The Australian Awards Scholarship provides coverage of up to $30,000 per year for tuition fees, living expenses, and other study-related costs. To be eligible, applicants must be refugee students from a participating country, have a minimum GPA of 3.5, and meet the English language proficiency requirements. The deadline for applications is 30 April 2026, and further information can be found at https://www.australiaawards.gov.au.
### Canada Graduate Scholarship
The Canada Graduate Scholarship provides coverage of up to $17,500 per year for master's and doctoral students. To be eligible, applicants must be refugee students enrolled in a STEM field at a Canadian university, have a minimum GPA of 3.5, and meet the language proficiency requirements. The deadline for applications is 1 December 2026, and further information can be found at https://www.nserc-crsng.gc.ca.
### International Student Scholarship
The International Student Scholarship provides coverage of up to $10,000 per year for undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, applicants must be refugee students enrolled in a STEM field at an Australian or Canadian university, have a minimum GPA of 3.0, and meet the language proficiency requirements. The deadline for applications is 31 March 2026, and further information can be found at https://www.studyinaustralia.gov.au.
### STEM Scholarship Program
The STEM Scholarship Program provides coverage of up to $20,000 per year for undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, applicants must be refugee students enrolled in a STEM field at a Canadian university, have a minimum GPA of 3.5, and meet the language proficiency requirements. The deadline for applications is 15 January 2026, and further information can be found at https://www.scholarships.nih.gov.
### University of Melbourne Scholarship
The University of Melbourne Scholarship provides coverage of up to $25,000 per year for undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, applicants must be refugee students enrolled in a STEM field at the University of Melbourne, have a minimum GPA of 3.5, and meet the language proficiency requirements. The deadline for applications is 31 October 2026, and further information can be found at https://www.unimelb.edu.au.

## Application Steps
To apply for the joint scholarship initiative, refugee students must follow these steps:
1. Check the eligibility criteria for each scholarship and ensure they meet the requirements.
2. Gather all required documents, including academic transcripts, language proficiency test results, and visa documents.
3. Choose the scholarships they wish to apply for and review the application requirements.
4. Submit their applications through the official website of each scholarship.
5. Ensure they meet the application deadlines for each scholarship.
6. Follow up on their application status and respond to any requests for additional information.
7. Accept the scholarship offer and complete any required paperwork.
8. Begin their studies in Australia or Canada and utilize the scholarship funding to support their education.

## Documents Checklist
To apply for the joint scholarship initiative, refugee students will need to provide the following documents:
* Academic transcripts
* Language proficiency test results (e.g. IELTS, TOEFL)
* Visa documents (e.g. passport, visa application)
* Proof of refugee status
* Personal statement or essay
* Letters of recommendation
* Proof of financial support
* Health insurance documents

## Frequently Asked Questions
What is the joint scholarship initiative for refugee students?
The joint scholarship initiative is a program launched by the Australian and Canadian governments to provide scholarships to refugee students pursuing STEM fields. The initiative aims to support the education and career development of refugee students in these fields, which are in high demand globally. Refugee students can apply for the initiative through the official website of each scholarship.
How do I apply for the joint scholarship initiative?
To apply for the joint scholarship initiative, refugee students must submit their applications through the official website of each scholarship. They must ensure they meet the eligibility criteria and application deadlines for each scholarship.
What are the eligibility criteria for the joint scholarship initiative?
The eligibility criteria for the joint scholarship initiative vary depending on the scholarship. However, most scholarships require refugee students to have a minimum GPA of 3.0, meet the language proficiency requirements, and be enrolled in a STEM field at an Australian or Canadian university.
Can I apply for multiple scholarships?
Yes, refugee students can apply for multiple scholarships through the joint scholarship initiative. However, they must ensure they meet the eligibility criteria and application deadlines for each scholarship.
How much funding is available through the joint scholarship initiative?
The amount of funding available through the joint scholarship initiative varies depending on the scholarship. However, most scholarships provide coverage of up to $20,000 per year for tuition fees, living expenses, and other study-related costs.
What are the required documents for the joint scholarship initiative?
The required documents for the joint scholarship initiative include academic transcripts, language proficiency test results, visa documents, proof of refugee status, and personal statements or essays.
How do I know if I am eligible for the joint scholarship initiative?
To determine if they are eligible for the joint scholarship initiative, refugee students should review the eligibility criteria for each scholarship and ensure they meet the requirements. They can also contact the scholarship providers for more information.
What is the deadline for applying to the joint scholarship initiative?
The deadline for applying to the joint scholarship initiative varies depending on the scholarship. However, most scholarships have deadlines between March and October 2026.

## External Resources
For more information on the joint scholarship initiative, refugee students can visit the official government websites of Australia and Canada, such as https://www.australia.gov.au and https://www.canada.ca.
Refugee students can also visit the website of the University of Melbourne, a participating university in the joint scholarship initiative, at https://www.unimelb.edu.au.

For further information and updates on scholarships for migrant and refugee students, please visit https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog
